title: eremophila oppositifolia subsp. rubra
intro: Eremophila oppositifolia subsp. rubra is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the red kangaroo bush due to its distinctive red flowers and bushy habit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and support for the immune system. Eremophila oppositifolia subsp. rubra is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the leaves and stems of the plant used to create various herbal preparations. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits have been studied in Australia, where it is native, and have been found to be effective in treating a range of health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal issues.
